response:
# VERDICT: REVISE ## Overall Assessment This custom tabs component implements 80% of the WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern but has critical design gaps that will cause keyboard-only and screen reader users to get lost during navigation. The explicitly marked bugs (focus not moving) are confirmed as real accessibility blockers, plus there's a missing aria-controls/aria-labelledby binding between tabs and panels that breaks semantic relationships for assistive technology. ## Pre-commitment Predictions Before reading code, I predicted: - Focus management after arrow key navigation incomplete ✓ **CONFIRMED** (explicit bug comments) - Missing aria-controls on tabs referencing panel IDs ✓ **FOUND** - Missing aria-labelledby on panels referencing active tab ID ✓ **FOUND** - Panel binding issues with hidden attribute usage ⚠️ **PARTIAL ISSUE** ## Critical Findings ### 1. Focus Not Moving to Newly Active Tab (Keyboard Trap) ```jsx // src/ProductTabs.jsx:23-40 handleKeyDown(e, index) { // ... arrow key handling sets activeTab state but never moves focus } ``` **Evidence**: Lines 23-40 show `setActiveTab(newIndex)` is called on ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/Home/End keys, but no code calls `.focus()` or uses ref to move keyboard focus. | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | **WCAG/APG** | WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard (No), WAI-ARIA APG Tabs Pattern §4.3 | | **User Group Impacted** | Screen reader users, keyboard-only users | | **Actual Behavior** | Tab selection changes visually but focus stays on old tab button; user must manually tab to new content | | **Expected Behavior** | Focus should move to newly active tab after state change (WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern requirement) | This is a CRITICAL issue because keyboard users cannot navigate between tabs without manual intervention. The WAI-ARIA APG explicitly requires focus management on selection changes for the roving tabindex pattern to work correctly. ### 2. Missing aria-controls/aria-labelledby Binding ```jsx // src/ProductTabs.jsx:48-57 (tab button) and lines 60-73 (panel div)
...
``` **Evidence**: - Tab buttons have `id` but no corresponding `aria-controls` attribute (lines 48) - Panel divs have `id` and `role="tabpanel"` but NO `aria-labelledby` referencing the active tab
